<!-- Source: https://www.chebfun.org/examples/geom/Ellipse.html -->

<div class="chebfun-import chebfun-example-import">
<div class='page-header'>
<h1>Perimeter of an ellipse</h1>
<h2>Nick Hale and Nick Trefethen, December 2010 in <a href='../'>geom</a><span><a href='/examples/geom/Ellipse.m'>download</a><span class='sep-sm'>&middot;</span><a href='//github.com/chebfun/examples/blob/master/geom/Ellipse.m'>view on GitHub</a></span></h2>
</div>

<div id='content' class="col-sm-12 example" role="main">
<p>The ellipse we use is the one used by Poisson in his paper of 1827 [1], with semiaxis lengths $0.5/\pi$ and $0.4/\pi$. We know thanks to M. Poisson that the perimeter is $0.902779927$ [*].</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">exact = 0.90277992777219;</pre>

<p>We now attempt to recompute this value using Chebfun.</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">theta = chebfun(@(theta) theta,[0,2*pi]);
x = (0.5/pi)*cos(theta);
y = (0.4/pi)*sin(theta);
plot(x,y,'-','LineWidth',2), axis equal
arc_length = norm(sqrt(diff(x).^2+diff(y).^2),1)</pre>

<pre class="mcode-output">arc_length =
   0.902779927772194
</pre>

<p><img src="../../../images/geom/Ellipse_01.png" class="figure chebfun-figure" alt=""></p>
<p>[*] Confusingly Poisson reported this number with a misprint in the 2nd place!</p>
<pre class="mcode">       "la valeur approchee de I sera I = 0,9927799272"</pre>

<h3 id="references">References</h3>
<ol>
<li>S.-D. Poisson, Sur le calcul numerique des integrales definies,    Memoires de L'Academie Royale des Sciences de L'Institut de France 4 (1827),    pp. 571-602 (written in 1823).</li>
</ol></div>
        </div>
</div>
